connections.rationale: Floating structures provide novel hard substrate for Mediterranean biofouling communities including invasive species that thrive in warm waters

connections.from: Climate change mitigation under Paris Agreement
connections.to: Floating offshore wind farm deployment and operation
connections.rationale: Mediterranean countries' Paris Agreement commitments and EU REPowerEU targets drive floating wind deployment to access deep-water resources close to demand centres

connections.from: Tourism and recreation seascape value
connections.to: Coastal property values and aesthetic perception
connections.rationale: Mediterranean coastal seascape aesthetics directly influence property values and tourism attractiveness, with wind farm visibility potentially reducing perceived value

connections.from: Anchor damage to Posidonia oceanica meadows from cable routes
connections.to: Coastal water quality and bathing water standards
connections.rationale: Degradation of Posidonia meadows reduces their capacity for water filtration and sediment stabilisation, potentially affecting coastal bathing water quality

connections.from: Coastal water quality and bathing water standards
connections.to: Tourism and recreation seascape value
connections.rationale: Clean coastal water quality is essential for Mediterranean beach tourism and directly determines bathing water classification under the EU Bathing Water Directive

connections.from: Displacement of artisanal fishers from wind farm zones
connections.to: De facto marine refuge from fishing exclusion zones
connections.rationale: Fishing exclusion in Mediterranean wind farm zones creates de facto marine refuges that allow overexploited demersal and reef fish populations to recover

connections.from: De facto marine refuge from fishing exclusion zones
connections.to: Artisanal fishery provisioning from coastal waters
connections.rationale: Marine refuge zones can produce spillover effects that benefit adjacent artisanal fisheries through increased fish abundance and biomass

connections.from: Deep-water wind resource potential close to Mediterranean coasts
connections.to: Floating offshore wind farm deployment and operation
connections.rationale: Mediterranean bathymetry drops steeply to >100m close to shore, making floating platforms the only viable technology to exploit excellent wind resources

connections.from: Environmental Impact Assessment with Posidonia protection
connections.to: Environmental monitoring and baseline surveys
connections.rationale: Mediterranean EIA frameworks require comprehensive environmental baseline surveys including Posidonia mapping, cetacean monitoring, and seabird census before consenting

connections.from: Entanglement risk from mooring lines for cetaceans and turtles
connections.to: Loggerhead sea turtle (Caretta caretta) population
connections.rationale: Loggerhead sea turtles are vulnerable to entanglement in mooring lines and dynamic cables, particularly during migration through Mediterranean floating wind zones

connections.from: Pelagic fish stocks (bluefin tuna, swordfish, anchovy)
connections.to: Pelagic fish aggregation around floating structures (FAD effect)
connections.rationale: Floating wind structures function as fish aggregation devices in the Mediterranean, attracting pelagic species including juvenile bluefin tuna and anchovy

connections.from: Pelagic fish aggregation around floating structures (FAD effect)
connections.to: Artisanal fisher household income and livelihood security
connections.rationale: Fish aggregation near floating structures may enhance catch rates for artisanal fishers if co-existence access agreements allow fishing in adjacent zones

connections.from: Overfishing pressure on demersal and reef fish
connections.to: Pelagic fish stocks (bluefin tuna, swordfish, anchovy)
connections.rationale: Overfishing of pelagic species (particularly bluefin tuna) combined with bycatch of juvenile pelagic fish in demersal fisheries reduces stock biomass

connections.from: Artisanal fishing tradition and livelihood needs
connections.to: Pelagic longline fishing for tuna and swordfish
connections.rationale: Mediterranean pelagic longline fishing for tuna and swordfish is driven by traditional livelihood needs and high market value of target species

connections.from: Pelagic longline fishing for tuna and swordfish
connections.to: Entanglement risk from mooring lines for cetaceans and turtles
connections.rationale: Displaced longline fisheries operating near wind farm boundaries may increase interactions between fishing gear, mooring lines, cetaceans and turtles

connections.from: Carbon emission reduction from Mediterranean floating wind
connections.to: Social acceptance and public perception of offshore wind
connections.rationale: Climate benefits of floating wind contribute to social acceptance, though visual impact, noise, and fisheries displacement can reduce public support

connections.from: Stakeholder engagement with fishing and tourism sectors
connections.to: Displacement of artisanal fishers from wind farm zones
connections.rationale: Effective stakeholder engagement with fishing and tourism sectors can mitigate displacement impacts through co-existence frameworks, compensation, and adaptive zoning
